name: Support chat: login outage escalation
description: A live customer-support chat. The coached user is the SUPPORT AGENT; whispers privately coach the AGENT. Designed to fire every agent in the Support suite and to showcase event coordination (question->answer in Phase 2), cooldown gating on the high-priority Escalation Alert, a sentiment variable that flips negative then positive, and an SLA Idle Monitor that fires only on a silence tick.
user_context: You are Sam, a Tier-1 support agent at Northwind Software. You are in a live chat with a customer. The copilot whispers private coaching to you -- the customer never sees it.

steps.speaker: AGENT
steps.text: Hi, you've reached Northwind support. I'm Sam -- what can I help you with today?
steps.note: Greeting opens. Phase Tracker sets phase=greeting (turn 1-2 rule); Sentiment Tracker writes sentiment=neutral (nothing positive/negative in this line). No whisper expected -- silence/event agents are skipped as trigger_type_mismatch this turn.

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: Hey. I can't log in to my account. It just keeps saying 'invalid credentials' even though my password is right.
steps.note: Product problem described. Expect: phase -> troubleshooting; KB Suggester fires a SUGGESTION (login/password KB article). sentiment stays neutral.

steps.speaker: AGENT
steps.text: Sorry about that. Let's get you back in. Have you tried resetting your password from the login screen?
steps.note: Agent responds. Quiet turn -- nothing keyword-worthy in the AGENT's own line (KB keywords aren't here), so no whisper.

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: Yes, I tried the reset twice. Why does this keep happening every single time I log in?
steps.note: A customer QUESTION ('?') about the login issue. Expect: Question Detector emits the `customer_question` event in Phase 1; Answer Suggester fires in PHASE 2 (one-hop coordination). KB Suggester also matches 'log in' (its cooldown=4s elapsed since t=11).

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: This is honestly ridiculous. I'm getting really frustrated -- I want a refund for this month.
steps.note: FIRST escalation. Expect: phase -> escalation; sentiment -> negative; Escalation Alert WARNING (priority 10), an `escalation_risk` fact, and escalation_count -> 1 in memory.

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: If you can't fix it I'll just cancel the whole subscription.
steps.note: SECOND escalation only 8s later. Escalation Alert is on a 12s cooldown -> it is GATED (look under 'skipped: cooldown'), so no new WARNING and escalation_count stays 1. phase stays escalation ('cancel'). Note sentiment flips back to NEUTRAL here: the mock only sees this last line, which has no explicit anger word -- a pointed lesson that per-turn classifiers are pure functions of the rendered prompt, not the running mood.

steps.speaker: AGENT
steps.text: I completely understand the frustration, and I'm going to get this sorted with you right now.
steps.note: Agent de-escalates (following the earlier whisper). Quiet turn -- no customer anger keywords in this AGENT line, so Escalation Alert does not even match here.

18s of dead air while the customer fumes. This turn is trigger=silence: ONLY the SLA Idle Monitor subscribes to SILENCE, so it fires a WARNING check-in. Every other agent shows under 'skipped: trigger_type_mismatch'. Contrast with the sales demo, where no silence agent existed and the equivalent turn fired nothing.

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: Fine, I'm still here. Are you actually able to fix the login today or not?
steps.note: Customer returns with another QUESTION. Expect Question Detector -> Answer Suggester again in Phase 2, plus a KB Suggester SUGGESTION (the line says 'login'). Escalation Alert does NOT fire -- no anger/refund/cancel word here. sentiment resolves to neutral (last line has no positive/negative token).

steps.speaker: AGENT
steps.text: Yes -- I just cleared the lock on your account. Please try logging in once more for me.
steps.note: Agent gives a direct answer + fix. Quiet turn for whispers.

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: Oh -- that worked! I'm in now. Thank you, I really appreciate you sticking with it.
steps.note: Resolution. Expect: phase -> resolution; sentiment -> positive. No warning/suggestion -- the happy path. A clean view of the variable trackers landing on their best values.

steps.speaker: CUSTOMER
steps.text: Actually, one more thing -- the cancel and refund I mentioned earlier, can we forget about that?
steps.note: Final turn. 'cancel'/'refund' appear AND it's a question. Escalation cooldown (12s) elapsed long ago, so Escalation Alert fires AGAIN (escalation_count -> 2) -- showing cooldown RECOVERY after the earlier gate. Question Detector -> Answer Suggester also fires in Phase 2. Note priority 10 escalation vs priority 2 answer both surface.
